name: Spider-Man
description: Peter Parker, Your Friendly neighborhood Spider-Man

name: Mary Jane Watson
description: Spider-Man/Peter Parker's long-time love interest

name: Gwen Stacy
description: The late love interest of Spider-Man/Peter Parker

name: Harry Osborn
description: Peter Parker's best friend and eventual foe

name: Liz Allen
description: Peter Parker's high school crush

name: Flash Thompson
description: Peter Parker's high school bully and eventual friend

name: Aunt May
description: Peter Parker's loving aunt

name: Uncle Ben
description: Peter Parker's late uncle

name: Crusher Hogan
description: The wrestler that

name: J. Jonah Jameson
description: The editor-in-chief of the Daily Bugle

name: Betty Brant
description: J. Jonah Jameson's secretary and occasional love interest of Peter Parker

name: John Jameson
description: World renowned astronaut and son of J. Jonah Jameson. Later becomes Man Wolf

name: Chameleon
description: A supervillain known for his ability to change his appearance. Brother of Kraven the Hunter

name: Norman Osborn
description: CEO of Oscorp. The Green Goblin. One of Spider-Man's greatest foes

name: Doctor Octopus
description: Otto Octavius, A brilliant scientist with four mechanical arms fused to his body

name: Vulture
description: Adrian Toomes, An elderly engineer who uses a special harness to fly and commit crime

name: Tinkerer
description: Phineas Mason, A brilliant inventor who creates gadgets for supervillains

name: Sandman
description: Flint Marko, a criminal who gained the ability to transform into sand

name: Burglar
description: The man responsible for the murder of Uncle Ben

name: Black Cat
description: The Black Cat, one of Spider-Man's most puzzling love interests

name: Doctor Doom
description: Victor Von Doom, ruler of Latveria and one of Marvel's greatest villains

name: Lizard
description: Dr. Curt Connors, a scientist who transforms into a reptilian monster

name: Living Brain
description: A powerful robot built by International Computing Machines Corporation

name: Electro
description: Maxwell Dillon, a villain with the ability to generate and control electricity

name: Big Man
description: Frederick Foswell, a Daily Bugle reporter who secretly led the Enforcers as the Big Man

name: Enforcers
description: A gang of criminals consisting of Ox, Montana, and Fancy Dan

name: Mysterio
description: Quentin Beck, a special effects artist turned master of illusion and supervillain

name: Kraven the Hunter
description: Sergei Kravinoff, the world's greatest hunter obsessed with defeating Spider-Man

name: Daredevil
description: Matt Murdock, a blind lawyer who fights crime as the Man Without Fear

name: Ringmaster
description: Maynard Tiboldt, leader of the Circus of Crime

name: Scorpion
description: Mac Gargan, a private investigator transformed into the Scorpion by J. Jonah Jameson

name: Beetle
description: Abner Jenkins, a mechanic who became a supervillain using a stolen suit of armor

name: Crime Master
description: Nick Lewis, a crime lord who clashed with Spider-Man and the Green Goblin

name: Molten Man
description: Mark Raxton, Liz Allen's step-brother who was accidentally coated in a liquid metal alloy

name: Spider-Slayer
description: A series of robots built by Spencer Smythe and commissioned by J. Jonah Jameson to destroy Spider-Man

name: Cat Burglar
description: A thief who appeared in early Spider-Man stories

name: Master Planner
description: Doctor Octopus operating under a secret alias to steal technology

name: Looter
description: Norton G. Fester, a villain who gained super strength from a meteor

name: Rhino
description: Aleksei Sytsevich, a criminal bonded to a rhinoceros-like suit giving him immense strength

name: Shocker
description: Herman Schultz, a villain who uses vibro-shock gauntlets to commit crimes

name: Blackie Drago
description: A criminal who stole the Vulture's harness while they were both imprisoned

name: Kingpin
description: Wilson Fisk, the powerful crime lord of New York City

name: Joe Robertson
description: Joseph 'Robbie' Robertson, the editor of the Daily Bugle and a close friend of Peter Parker

name: Captain Stacy
description: George Stacy, a retired NYPD captain and father of Gwen Stacy

name: Ka-Zar
description: Kevin Plunder, a lord of the jungle who rules over the Savage Land

name: Quicksilver
description: Pietro Maximoff, a mutant with the ability to move at superhuman speeds

name: Silvermane
description: Silvio Manfredi, an aging Maggia crime boss obsessed with regaining his youth

name: Prowler
description: Hobie Brown, a young inventor who created a suit to commit crimes before becoming a hero

name: Kangaroo
description: Frank Oliver, a villain with kangaroo-like agility and strength

name: Schemer
description: Richard Fisk, the son of the Kingpin who disguised himself to take down his father

name: Black Widow
description: Natasha Romanoff, a former Soviet spy turned S.H.I.E.L.D. agent and Avenger

name: Iceman
description: Bobby Drake, a mutant with the ability to generate and control ice

name: Sha Shan
description: A Vietnamese woman connected to Flash Thompson's past in the Vietnam War

name: Doctor Strange
description: Stephen Strange, the Sorcerer Supreme and master of the mystic arts

name: Gibbon
description: Martin Blank, a man with ape-like abilities who was manipulated by Kraven the Hunter

name: Hammerhead
description: A Maggia crime boss with a steel-reinforced skull who uses his head as a weapon

name: Smasher
description: A villain who appeared in the political storyline issues of Amazing Spider-Man

name: Disruptor
description: A politically motivated villain who clashed with Spider-Man in the early 1970s

name: Hulk
description: Bruce Banner, a scientist who transforms into a green-skinned giant of immense strength

name: Luke Cage
description: A street-level hero with unbreakable skin and superhuman strength, also known as Power Man

name: Man-Wolf
description: John Jameson transformed into a werewolf-like creature by a mystical moonstone

name: Punisher
description: Frank Castle, a vigilante who wages a one-man war on crime after the murder of his family

name: Jackal
description: Miles Warren, a geneticist and former professor of Peter Parker who became obsessed with cloning

name: Tarantula
description: A South American mercenary with poisoned toe spikes on his boots

name: Mindworm
description: William Turner, a mutant with powerful telepathic abilities

name: Grizzly
description: Maxwell Markham, a criminal who wears a bear-like exoskeleton suit

name: Cyclone
description: André Gerard, a French criminal who uses a suit that generates powerful wind vortexes

name: Spider-Clone
description: Ben Reilly, the clone of Peter Parker created by the Jackal

name: Medusa
description: Medusalith Amaquelin, queen of the Inhumans with prehensile hair

name: Morbius
description: Dr. Michael Morbius, a biochemist turned living vampire after a failed experiment

name: Mister Fantastic
description: Reed Richards, The leader of the Fantastic Four

name: Invisible Woman
description: Susan Storm, First lady of Marvel and of the Fantastic Four

name: Human Torch
description: Johnny Storm, The hot headed younger brother Sue Storm

name: Thing
description: Ben Grimm, The strongest member of the Fantastic Four

name: Batman
description: The Dark Knight of Gotham

name: Invincible
description: Teenage Viltumite

name: Hellboy
description: Half-demon from the B.P.R.D.
